Container.Name: Restore-Runner
Container.Overview:
Continuous backup verification for restic repos.
RestoreRunner captures a point-in-time baseline of your source files (path, size, mtime, SHA-256). On a schedule it samples N files from the backup, pulls them via restic, and hashes them byte-for-byte against the baseline. Tells you whether your backup is restorable, or silently rotting.
Features: - Zero-config first run: open the web UI, set an admin, add a repo - Captures baselines on enrollment (rsync-empty-folder safe — compares against a fixed snapshot, not the live source) - Structural check catches source collapse even when every sampled file still matches its baseline - Apprise notifications on fail / degraded - Per-repo + fleet CSV export of rehearsal history
v0.1 supports restic only. Borg + Kopia coming in v0.2.
First-run: - Open the WebUI — wizard asks you to create an admin - Add a backup repo (URL, password, source path) - The baseline capture runs in the background
Pre-release — report issues on GitHub.
Container.DonateText: If you like my work, please consider a donation!

Config.Name: Config Dir
Config.Description: Stores the SQLite DB, master encryption key, and baselines. Do not delete without intention — losing this means re-enrolling every repo.

Config.Name: Data (read-only)
Config.Description: Mount your backup source directory as read-only. RestoreRunner only reads from here (to walk the baseline + run structural checks). Add more of these for additional source directories.
